verses.text: In that day they sing of a strong city whose walls are salvation; 'thou wilt keep him in perfect peace, whose mind is stayed on thee.' Trust brings peace. The heart fixed on God rests secure amid every storm.

In that day they sing of a strong city whose walls are salvation; 'thou wilt keep him in perfect peace, whose mind is stayed on thee.' Trust brings peace. The heart fixed on God rests secure amid every storm.

In that day they sing of a strong city whose walls are salvation; 'thou wilt keep him in perfect peace, whose mind is stayed on thee.' Trust brings peace. The heart fixed on God rests secure amid every storm.

'Trust ye in the LORD for ever: for in the LORD JEHOVAH is everlasting strength'; He brings down the lofty city to the dust. He is an unfailing rock. Lasting strength is found only in leaning on the eternal God.

'Trust ye in the LORD for ever: for in the LORD JEHOVAH is everlasting strength'; He brings down the lofty city to the dust. He is an unfailing rock. Lasting strength is found only in leaning on the eternal God.

'Trust ye in the LORD for ever: for in the LORD JEHOVAH is everlasting strength'; He brings down the lofty city to the dust. He is an unfailing rock. Lasting strength is found only in leaning on the eternal God.
The way of the just is level, and 'with my soul have I desired thee in the night,' seeking God early. The godly long for Him. The believer's deepest desire, even in darkness, is for God Himself.
The way of the just is level, and 'with my soul have I desired thee in the night,' seeking God early. The godly long for Him. The believer's deepest desire, even in darkness, is for God Himself.
The way of the just is level, and 'with my soul have I desired thee in the night,' seeking God early. The godly long for Him. The believer's deepest desire, even in darkness, is for God Himself.

Though favour is shown the wicked, he 'will not learn righteousness,' blind to the LORD's uplifted hand. Mercy alone does not soften the hard. Some only harden under the kindness that should melt them.

Though favour is shown the wicked, he 'will not learn righteousness,' blind to the LORD's uplifted hand. Mercy alone does not soften the hard. Some only harden under the kindness that should melt them.
'LORD, thou wilt ordain peace for us,' they pray, owning that He works all their works and has enlarged the nation. Peace is His gift. Whatever good His people accomplish, He has wrought it in them.

'LORD, thou wilt ordain peace for us,' they pray, owning that He works all their works and has enlarged the nation. Peace is His gift. Whatever good His people accomplish, He has wrought it in them.

In trouble they cried, and now comes the promise: 'thy dead men shall live... Awake and sing, ye that dwell in dust.' Here resurrection dawns. The God who raises the dead is the hope of His suffering people.

In trouble they cried, and now comes the promise: 'thy dead men shall live... Awake and sing, ye that dwell in dust.' Here resurrection dawns. The God who raises the dead is the hope of His suffering people.

'Come, my people... hide thyself for a little moment, until the indignation be overpast,' for the LORD comes to punish the earth's sin. Shelter is offered in judgment. God hides His own while He visits the wicked.
